_schema.description: Inventory of all documents under research. Each document has source-language transcription and translations into the four working languages.

title.en: Displaced Persons (DIPIS) card — David Rapaport, Brussels 1946
title.he: כרטיס עקורים — דוד רפפורט, בריסל 1946
title.pl: Karta DIPIS — Dawid Rapaport, Bruksela 1946
title.fr: Fiche DIPIS — David Rapaport, Bruxelles 1946

documents.source_archive: Belgian Repatriation Commission, Ministry of Public Health and Family (later stamp 1969). Originally a DIPIS file from teczka DP3299, naming range RANKIS-RAPAPORT.

transcription_notes.en: Pre-printed English form, filled in by hand in French. Some words stained/illegible. Team decoding: Basia, Wojtek (Polish), Kasia (French expert), Róża.
transcription_notes.fr: Formulaire pré-imprimé en anglais, rempli à la main en français. Certains mots tachés/illisibles.

decoded_fields.name: Rapaport, David
decoded_fields.languages_fluent: a) Polish (native) b) Italian, German c) Czech, Russian

translations.en: See decoded_fields. The 'B.' is Belgium/Brussels. The mother/father names and Cieszyn birthplace differ from family knowledge — explained by clerk's transcription error per the 15 May Nadwórna finding.
translations.he: ראה decoded_fields. ה'ב.' הוא בלגיה/בריסל. שמות ההורים ומקום הלידה צ'שין שונים מהידע המשפחתי — מוסבר כשגיאת תעתיק של פקיד לפי הממצא מ-15 במאי בנדבורנא.
translations.pl: Patrz decoded_fields. 'B.' to Belgia/Bruksela. Imiona rodziców i miejsce urodzenia Cieszyn różnią się od wiedzy rodzinnej — wyjaśnione jako błąd transkrypcji urzędnika zgodnie z odkryciem z 15 maja w Nadwórnej.
translations.fr: Voir decoded_fields. Le 'B.' est Belgique/Bruxelles. Les noms des parents et le lieu de naissance Cieszyn diffèrent de ce que connaît la famille — expliqué comme erreur de transcription du fonctionnaire, suite à la découverte du 15 mai à Nadwórna.

open_questions: Verify David's handwritten signature against any Israeli document (customs records?). Family has been unable to locate his English/Hebrew signature so far.
Decipher the stained note next to 1942 — 'deforce' (Belgian French for 'deprived') or 'déporté' (deported)?
Why did the clerk write 'Tesin'? Mishearing 'Nadwórna'? Or perhaps David himself misstated, given the political situation?

title.en: Birth record — Dawid Mendel Rapaport, Nadwórna 1911
title.he: תעודת לידה — דוד מנדל רפפורט, נדבורנא 1911
title.pl: Akt urodzenia — Dawid Mendel Rapaport, Nadwórna 1911
title.fr: Acte de naissance — Dawid Mendel Rapaport, Nadwórna 1911

documents.source_archive: Jewish Historical Institute (Żydowski Instytut Historyczny / ŻIH), Warsaw — Bolechów district records / Galician Jewish vital records

translations.en: Per Ms. Kasia (ŻIH): 'Dawid Rapaport's parents are Berisz Rapaport and Rebeka Griffel, daughter of Leizor Griffel and Sara née Chajes, merchants of Nadwórna.' Full transcription pending.
translations.he: לפי גב' קאסיה (ŻIH): 'הוריו של דוד רפפורט הם בעריש (ברנרד) רפפורט ורבקה גריפל, בתם של ליזר גריפל ושרה לבית חיות, סוחרים בנדבורנא.' תמלול מלא ממתין.
translations.pl: Według Pani Kasi (ŻIH): 'Rodzice Dawida Rapaporta to Berisz Rapaport i Rebeka Griffel, córka Leizora Griffela i Sary z domu Chajes, kupców z Nadwórnej.' Pełny zapis w trakcie opracowania.
translations.fr: Selon Mme Kasia (ŻIH) : « Les parents de Dawid Rapaport sont Berisz Rapaport et Rebeka Griffel, fille de Leizor Griffel et Sara née Chajes, commerçants de Nadwórna. » Transcription complète en attente.

open_questions: Full transcription of the German Gothic script (Basia is reviewing)

title.en: Birth record — Rebeka Griffel, Nadwórna 1888
title.he: תעודת לידה — רבקה גריפל, נדבורנא 1888
title.pl: Akt urodzenia — Rebeka Griffel, Nadwórna 1888
title.fr: Acte de naissance — Rebeka Griffel, Nadwórna 1888

documents.source_archive: Jewish Historical Institute (ŻIH), Warsaw — found by Ms. Kasia, 15 May 2026

translations.en: In German Gothic script. Basia is working on the full reading. Confirms: father Leizor Griffel, mother Sara née Chajes, merchants of Nadwórna.
translations.he: בכתב גרמני גותי. בסיה עובדת על הקריאה המלאה. מאשרת: אב — ליזר גריפל, אם — שרה לבית חיות, סוחרים בנדבורנא.
translations.pl: Pismo niemieckie gotyckie. Basia pracuje nad pełnym odczytem. Potwierdza: ojciec Leizor Griffel, matka Sara z domu Chajes, kupcy z Nadwórnej.
translations.fr: Écriture gothique allemande. Basia travaille sur la lecture complète. Confirme : père Leizor Griffel, mère Sara née Chajes, commerçants de Nadwórna.

title.en: Birth record — Lea Weitzner, Bolechów 1913
title.he: תעודת לידה — לאה וייצנר, בולוכוב 1913
title.pl: Akt urodzenia — Lea Weitzner, Bolechów 1913
title.fr: Acte de naissance — Léa Weitzner, Bolechów 1913

documents.source_archive: Bolechów Jewish vital records, AGAD Warsaw (former Eastern Borderlands collection) — gmina wyznaniowa Bolechów, powiat Dolina, księga metrykalna urodzeń 1907-1918

translations.en: Lea Weitzner, born 8 April 1913 in Bolechów. Father Elias Weitzner (cattle dealer), mother Matel née Weinreb. Given her name in the Bolechów synagogue on 12 April. Mother's parents: Samuel Weinreb (tavern keeper in Dolina) and Ester Blima. Elias and Matel married in Dolina, 1905 (statutory marriage).
translations.he: לאה וייצנר, נולדה 8 באפריל 1913 בבולוכוב. אב — אליהו וייצנר (סוחר בקר), אם — מתל לבית ויינרב. ניתן לה שם בבית הכנסת בבולוכוב ב-12 באפריל. הורי האם: שמואל ויינרב (בעל פונדק בדולינה) ואסתר בלימה. אליהו ומתל נישאו בדולינה ב-1905 (נישואין אזרחיים).
translations.pl: Lea Weitzner, urodzona 8 kwietnia 1913 w Bolechowie. Ojciec Elias Weitzner (handlarz bydłem), matka Matel z domu Weinreb. Imię nadane w synagodze w Bolechowie 12 kwietnia. Rodzice matki: Samuel Weinreb (karczmarz w Dolinie) i Ester Blima. Elias i Matel zawarli ślub w Dolinie w 1905 roku (małżeństwo statutowe).
translations.fr: Léa Weitzner, née le 8 avril 1913 à Bolechów. Père Elias Weitzner (marchand de bétail), mère Matel née Weinreb. Nommée à la synagogue de Bolechów le 12 avril. Parents de la mère : Samuel Weinreb (tavernier à Dolina) et Ester Blima. Elias et Matel se sont mariés à Dolina en 1905 (mariage civil).

title.en: Birth record — Feige (Tzipora) Weitzner, 1911
title.he: תעודת לידה — פייגה (ציפורה) וייצנר, 1911
title.pl: Akt urodzenia — Feige (Tzipora) Weitzner, 1911
title.fr: Acte de naissance — Feige (Tzipora) Weitzner, 1911

documents.source_archive: Bolechów Jewish vital records (AGAD Warsaw)

translations.en: Confirms: Feige Weitzner, born 1911 in Bolechów, daughter of Elias Weitzner (cattle dealer) and Matel née Weinreb. Family memory confirms Feige is the Yiddish name of Tzipora (Hebrew).
translations.he: מאשרת: פייגה וייצנר, נולדה 1911 בבולוכוב, בתם של אליהו וייצנר (סוחר בקר) ומתל לבית ויינרב. הזיכרון המשפחתי מאשר שפייגה זה שמה היידי של ציפורה (עברית).
translations.pl: Potwierdza: Feige Weitzner, ur. 1911 w Bolechowie, córka Eliasa Weitznera (handlarza bydłem) i Matel z domu Weinreb. Pamięć rodzinna potwierdza, że Feige to imię jidisz dla Tzipora (hebrajskie).
translations.fr: Confirme : Feige Weitzner, née en 1911 à Bolechów, fille d'Elias Weitzner (marchand de bétail) et Matel née Weinreb. La mémoire familiale confirme que Feige est le prénom yiddish de Tzipora (hébreu).

title.en: Birth record — Moses (Mojzesz) Weitzner, 1916
title.he: תעודת לידה — משה וייצנר, 1916
title.pl: Akt urodzenia — Mojzesz Weitzner, 1916
title.fr: Acte de naissance — Moïse Weitzner, 1916

documents.source_archive: Bolechów Jewish vital records (AGAD Warsaw)

translations.en: Moses (Mojzesz) Weitzner born 1916 in Bolechów, to Elias Weitzner (cattle dealer) and Matel née Weinreb.
translations.he: משה (מויז'ש) וייצנר נולד 1916 בבולוכוב, לאליהו וייצנר (סוחר בקר) ומתל לבית ויינרב.
translations.pl: Mojzesz Weitzner ur. 1916 w Bolechowie, Eliasa Weitznera (handlarza bydłem) i Matel z domu Weinreb.
translations.fr: Moïse (Mojzesz) Weitzner né en 1916 à Bolechów, d'Elias Weitzner (marchand de bétail) et Matel née Weinreb.

title.en: CKŻP post-war registration — Szymon Rapaport (1937)
title.he: רישום הוועד המרכזי של יהודי פולין — שמעון רפפורט (1937)
title.pl: Rejestracja CKŻP — Szymon Rapaport (1937)
title.fr: Enregistrement CKŻP — Szymon Rapaport (1937)

documents.source_archive: Central Committee of Polish Jews (Centralny Komitet Żydów w Polsce, CKŻP), post-1945 survivor lists — indexed at Jewish Historical Institute (ŻIH), Warsaw

translations.en: Szymon Rappaport, son of Lusia and Dawid, year of birth 1937. The CKŻP collected names of Jewish survivors in Poland so families could find each other. Family confirms Shimon's actual DOB as 22.06.1937.
translations.he: שמעון רפפורט, בן לוסיה ודוד, שנת לידה 1937. הוועד המרכזי של יהודי פולין אסף שמות של ניצולים יהודים בפולין כדי שמשפחות יוכלו למצוא זה את זה. המשפחה מאשרת שתאריך הלידה האמיתי של שמעון הוא 22.06.1937.
translations.pl: Szymon Rappaport, syn Lusi i Dawida, rok urodzenia 1937. CKŻP zbierał nazwiska żydowskich ocalałych w Polsce, aby rodziny mogły się odnaleźć. Rodzina potwierdza rzeczywistą datę urodzenia Szymona jako 22.06.1937.
translations.fr: Szymon Rappaport, fils de Lusia et Dawid, année de naissance 1937. Le CKŻP collectait les noms des survivants juifs en Pologne pour que les familles puissent se retrouver. La famille confirme la date de naissance réelle de Szymon : 22.06.1937.

title.en: Polish press 1912 — Krynica guest register listing Regina Rapaport
title.he: עיתון פולני 1912 — רשימת אורחי קריניצה כולל את רגינה רפפורט
title.pl: Prasa polska 1912 — wykaz gości Krynicy z Reginą Rapaport
title.fr: Presse polonaise 1912 — registre des hôtes de Krynica avec Regina Rapaport

documents.source_archive: Polish digital press archive (NDIGCZAS), 1912 newspaper page 7

translations.en: On page 7, the newspaper reports that Regina Rapaport, wife of a merchant from Nadwórna, arrived alone at Krynica resort and registered at the 'Alfredówka' guesthouse on 25 July 1912. This independently confirms Rebeka Griffel as Regina Rapaport (married name), wife of David's father.
translations.he: בעמוד 7, העיתון מדווח שרגינה רפפורט, אשתו של סוחר מנדבורנא, הגיעה לבדה לעיר הנופש קריניצה ונרשמה באכסניית 'אלפרדובקה' ב-25 ביולי 1912. זה מאשר באופן עצמאי את רבקה גריפל כרגינה רפפורט (שם נישואין), אשתו של אביו של דוד.
translations.pl: Na stronie 7 gazeta informuje, że Regina Rapaport, żona kupca z Nadwórnej, przyjechała sama do uzdrowiska Krynica i zameldowała się w pensjonacie 'Alfredówka' 25 lipca 1912 roku. To niezależnie potwierdza Rebekę Griffel jako Reginę Rapaport (nazwisko po mężu), żonę ojca Dawida.
translations.fr: À la page 7, le journal rapporte que Regina Rapaport, épouse d'un commerçant de Nadwórna, est arrivée seule à la station thermale de Krynica et s'est inscrite à la pension 'Alfredówka' le 25 juillet 1912. Ceci confirme de manière indépendante que Rebeka Griffel est Regina Rapaport (nom d'épouse), femme du père de Dawid.

title.en: Descendants chart — Moshe Rapaport (Rapaport Research Institute)
title.he: תרשים צאצאי משה רפפורט (מכון מחקר רפפורט)
title.pl: Tablica potomków Moshe Rapaport (Instytut Badań Rapaportów)
title.fr: Tableau de descendance — Moshe Rapaport (Institut de Recherche Rapaport)

documents.source_archive: Rapaport Research Institute, Jerusalem (per Doron, 06 May)

translations.en: A descendants tree of Moshe & Menucha Rapaport listing many family members tied to 'Mosina'. This chart triggered the original assumption that Mosina (near Poznań) was the family seat. Doron notes it could not be verified anywhere else and the data may be mixed with unrelated branches.
translations.he: עץ צאצאים של משה ומנוחה רפפורט המונה רבים מבני המשפחה הקשורים ל'מוסינה'. תרשים זה הוא שיצר את ההנחה המקורית שמוסינה (ליד פוזנן) הייתה מושב המשפחה. דורון מציין שלא ניתן היה לאמת זאת בשום מקום אחר ושהנתונים עשויים להיות מעורבבים עם ענפים לא קשורים.
translations.pl: Drzewo potomków Moshe i Menuchi Rapaport wymieniające wielu członków rodziny powiązanych z 'Mosiną'. Ten wykres wywołał pierwotne założenie, że Mosina (pod Poznaniem) była siedzibą rodziny. Doron zaznacza, że nie udało się tego nigdzie indziej potwierdzić, a dane mogą być pomieszane z niepowiązanymi gałęziami.
translations.fr: Un arbre de descendance de Moshe et Menucha Rapaport mentionnant de nombreux membres liés à « Mosina ». Ce tableau a engendré l'hypothèse initiale que Mosina (près de Poznań) était le siège familial. Doron note qu'il n'a pu être vérifié nulle part ailleurs et que les données peuvent être mélangées avec des branches non liées.

title.en: Research report — Basia, 13 May 2026
title.he: דו״ח מחקר — בסיה, 13 במאי 2026
title.pl: Raport badawczy — Basia, 13 maja 2026
title.fr: Rapport de recherche — Basia, 13 mai 2026

translations.en: Basia's findings: (1) Found 'Rapaport S. i Liptan M.' sawmill in Wierzbiany (1930), connection unverified. (2) Difficulty with Polish declension of place names (Lewów = Lwów). (3) Floats hypothesis 'Mosina' = 'Dolina' (same letters/ending). Notes Dolina had multiple sawmills in 1930. (4) Searched Przemyśl: Bernard Rapaport appears around 1910. (5) Found 'Lusia Rapaport' in the Jewish Historical Institute (ŻIH) catalogs — index lists Lusia Rappoport born 1916-04-08, parents Eljan and Mahylda. Will request materials from Warsaw. (6) Confirms Vitsner=Weitzner=Wizner. Weitzner family in Bolechów: Elias + Matel; daughter Lea 8.4.1913; sister Feige 1911. (7) Found SZYMON RAPPAPORT in ŻIH indexes, son of Lusia and Dawid, 1937 — certainly the uncle. (8) Recommends applying to IPN (6+ month wait). (9) Cieszyn records not online; available at Katowice archive. (10) Asks: did David serve in army? Polish September 1939 mobilization? Polish Army West? How did he escape USSR — Anders Army to Iran in 1942? How did Leah and Shimon survive — ghetto or Aryan side? Did they go to Krynica? Did they flee to Nadwórna family or were they deported?
translations.he: ממצאי בסיה: (1) נמצאה משרפה של 'רפפורט ש. וליפטן מ.' בוויירזביאני (1930), הקשר לא מאומת. (2) קשיים בנטיית שמות מקומות פולנית. (3) השערה: 'מוסינה' = 'דולינה'. (4) חיפוש בפשמישל: ברנרד רפפורט מופיע סביב 1910. (5) נמצאה 'לוסיה רפפורט' בקטלוגי המכון ההיסטורי היהודי — מצוין לידה 8.4.1916, הורים אליאן ומהילדה. (6) מאשרת Vitsner=Weitzner=Wizner. (7) נמצא שמעון רפפורט בקטלוג ŻIH. (8) ממליצה לפנות ל-IPN (6 חודשי המתנה). (9) רשומות צ'שין באר.כיון קטוביץ. (10) שאלות: שירת בצבא? כיצד יצא מברה״מ — צבא אנדרס לאיראן 1942? כיצד שרדו לאה ושמעון?
translations.pl: Pełny raport Basi w polskim oryginale — szczegóły w pliku PDF.
translations.fr: Conclusions de Basia : (1) Scierie 'Rapaport S. i Liptan M.' à Wierzbiany (1930), lien non vérifié. (2) Hypothèse 'Mosina' = 'Dolina'. (3) Bernard Rapaport apparaît à Przemyśl vers 1910. (4) Lusia Rapaport trouvée dans les catalogues de l'Institut historique juif (ŻIH). (5) Confirme Vitsner=Weitzner=Wizner. (6) Szymon Rapaport trouvé. (7) Recommande demande à l'IPN (attente 6+ mois). (8) Questions : armée polonaise, Armée Anders, comment Léa et Szymon ont survécu, Krynica ?

title.en: Research report — Basia, 15 May 2026 (Nadwórna breakthrough)
title.he: דו״ח מחקר — בסיה, 15 במאי 2026 (פריצת דרך נדבורנא)
title.pl: Raport badawczy — Basia, 15 maja 2026 (przełom Nadwórna)
title.fr: Rapport de recherche — Basia, 15 mai 2026 (percée Nadwórna)

translations.en: Ms. Kasia at ŻIH found Dawid's birth certificate IN NADWÓRNA — not Cieszyn. Parents Berisz Rapaport and Rebeka Griffel (daughter of Leizor Griffel and Sara née Chajes, merchants of Nadwórna). Also found Rebeka's birth certificate (1888, German Gothic script). 'Mosina' remains a mystery — Ms. Kasia hypothesizes Morszyn (?). Mszana Dolna and Dolina also under consideration. Open question: when Leah fled to Przemyśl after war began, was she fleeing to mother-in-law Regina/Rebeka? Logic suggests fleeing west from Soviets, then east from Germans, then west again as Yalta decided Lwów would remain Soviet. Bolechów records: Polish until end 1939, no records first half 1940, Cyrillic (Ukrainian-style) second half 1940, nothing after. The Weinreb family from Dolina — no records survive there. Ms. Kasia of ŻIH proposes Doron be added to direct email correspondence.
translations.he: גב' קאסיה ב-ŻIH מצאה את תעודת הלידה של דוד בנדבורנא — לא בצ'שין. הורים: ברנרד (בעריש) רפפורט ורבקה גריפל (בתם של ליזר גריפל ושרה לבית חיות, סוחרים בנדבורנא). גם נמצאה תעודת הלידה של רבקה (1888, כתב גרמני גותי). 'מוסינה' עדיין תעלומה — גב' קאסיה משערת מורשין (?). משאנה דולנה ודולינה גם הן בבחינה.
translations.pl: Pełny raport Basi w polskim oryginale.
translations.fr: Mme Kasia au ŻIH a trouvé l'acte de naissance de Dawid À NADWÓRNA — pas à Cieszyn. Parents : Berisz Rapaport et Rebeka Griffel (fille de Leizor Griffel et Sara née Chajes, commerçants de Nadwórna). Acte de naissance de Rebeka aussi trouvé (1888, écriture gothique allemande). « Mosina » reste un mystère — Mme Kasia émet l'hypothèse Morszyn (?).

title.en: Map: Bolechów / Dolina (Galicia)
title.he: מפה: בולוכוב / דולינה (גליציה)
title.pl: Mapa: Bolechów / Dolina (Galicja)
title.fr: Carte : Bolechów / Dolina (Galicie)

translations.en: Reference map showing Bolechów (birthplace of the Weitzner children) and Dolina (district seat, origin of the Weinreb family). The two are nearby in former eastern Galicia.
translations.he: מפה: בולוכוב (מקום לידת ילדי וייצנר) ודולינה (מושב המחוז, מקור משפחת ויינרב). השניים סמוכים בגליציה המזרחית לשעבר.
translations.pl: Mapa referencyjna: Bolechów (miejsce urodzenia dzieci Weitznerów) i Dolina (siedziba powiatu, pochodzenie rodziny Weinreb). Obie miejscowości blisko siebie w byłej Galicji Wschodniej.
translations.fr: Carte de référence : Bolechów (lieu de naissance des enfants Weitzner) et Dolina (chef-lieu du district, origine de la famille Weinreb). Les deux localités sont proches l'une de l'autre en ex-Galicie orientale.

title.en: The Story of Lusia (Sipura shel Lusia)
title.he: סיפורה של לוסיה
title.pl: Historia Lusi
title.fr: L'histoire de Lusia

documents.source_archive: Family — scanned PDF uploaded by Dalia, Dana, Doron and Daniel 20 May 2026. 90 pages, Hebrew, with embedded photos and dedication page. Compiled in 1986 by Esther Weiss based on Lusia Rapaport's first-person recollections, with extensive multi-session interviews recorded by Esther's pen and later edited by Moshe Goter.

summary.en: Lusia (Leah) Rapaport née Weitzner's memoir, dictated and compiled in 1986 by her granddaughter's teacher Esther Weiss. The primary historical document for Lusia's life from childhood in Bolechów through marriage in Muszyna (Feb 1938), survival under Nazi occupation in Lwów with false identities, escape with David and Shimon, post-war Brussels and the journey to Israel. Settled in Haifa, Moriah Street 93. OCR'd 2026-05-20 via Google Cloud Vision (~70k Hebrew chars). Translated to English and Polish by Claude Haiku 4.5 (batched). PRIMARY SOURCE for resolving H1 Mosina = Muszyna, and confirming David's wooden-wagon escape, Lota's existence, the Legionów 24 address, and the marriage date Feb 1938.

title.en: NKVD repressions against residents of the border zone, 1939-1941 (IPN research paper 6066)
title.he: דיכוי הנ.ק.ו.ד. נגד תושבי אזור הגבול, 1939-1941 (מאמר מחקר IPN 6066)
title.pl: Represje NKWD wobec mieszkańców strefy przygranicznej 1939-1941 (Praca naukowa IPN 6066)

documents.source_archive: Polish Institute of National Remembrance (IPN), shared by Basia 17 May 2026 via WhatsApp

summary.en: IPN academic paper covering Soviet repressions in the border zone with Germany 1939-1941. Includes a detailed section on 'Passportization' — the forced Soviet passport campaign that all residents of border towns like Przemyśl had to navigate. Basia shared this paper as historical CONTEXT — Leah was briefly in Soviet-occupied Przemyśl in 1939, and this paper documents what life under that occupation looked like for everyone living there. The paper also informs a planned formal IPN application for any Rapaport family records held by the Polish Institute of National Remembrance (see h_ipn_application). Reading this paper helps the family understand the conditions David and Leah lived through — both Soviet and Nazi occupations — before they were able to escape and reach Brussels in 1946.

summary.en: PARADIGM-SHIFTING DOCUMENT. This is the source of the 23 May 2026 breakthrough that cracked open the paternal Rapaport line. Before this discovery, even Edward Gelles (25+ years researching the Griffel-Nadwórna dynasty) had only the bare name 'Berish Rapaport' as Rivka Griffel's husband — no parents, no birthplace, no birth date. This 1924 passport application names Berisz's father (Mojżesz Saul Rapaport), his Tarnów origin address, his 1924 Stanisławów residence, and provides ages for both children (Mendel 12, Lotti 8 → confirms David b.1911 and Lota b.~1916). The photo (WA0002) shows resemblance to Dov (Dana's reaction).

summary.en: Birth certificate that confirms Berisz Rapaport's full origin in Tarnów (Western Galicia), his civil birth name 'Benzion', and identifies two further generations of ancestors: paternal grandfather Mojżesz Saul Rapaport (private teacher) and Schiff maternal-grandparents Michał + Cywie. Image not yet in family archive — Basia provided transcription only; obtain scan from Tarnów archive.

documents.source_archive: Shared by Basia 23 May 2026 via WhatsApp. Origin appears to be a family-built genealogy tool (Geni / MyHeritage style export with standard birth/death card layout). Built before the May 2026 Tarnów + Nadwórna breakthroughs — uses 'Mosina' as universal placeholder.

gen_2_children_7: Dov Rapaport (Mosina) — likely = Berisz/Berisch Rapaport (Hebrew 'Dov' = Yiddish 'Berisch' = bear)
Rivka [...] (Mosina, surname blank — likely a spouse who married in)
gen_3_via_dov_berisz_oo_unknown: Leah Lusha Vitsner (Stanisławów, †1996 Haifa) — David's wife
gen_3_via_avraham: Moshe Rapaport (*1910, †before 1993.01 Tel Aviv) — NEW LEAD: paternal first cousin of David, survived to Tel Aviv
gen_4_spouses: Tamar Bugin (*1942.04.23) oo Shimon 1967.04.17 Tel Aviv

summary.en: BREAKTHROUGH FAMILY TREE: Identifies the apical couple as MOSHE RAPAPORT + MENUCHA, with 7 children. If this Moshe is the same as Mojżesz Saul Rapaport of Tarnów (Berisz's father per the 1884 birth cert), then: (a) Menucha is the matriarch's NAME — but birth cert names Rywka Schiff. Likely two wives: Menucha first, Rywka second, with Berisz born to Rywka. OR Menucha is a Hebrew name and Rywka is the civil name. (b) 'Dov Rapaport' in Gen 2 = Berisz (Hebrew 'Dov' = 'Berisch' = bear), meaning Berisz had 6 SIBLINGS: Chava Eva, Malka Maltse, Shabtai, Rivka [...], Rivka, Avraham — all previously unknown to us and to Edward Gelles. (c) Avraham Rapaport's son Moshe (b.1910, d.<1993.01 Tel Aviv) is a paternal first cousin of David — a new searchable lead in Israeli archives. Pre-breakthrough family-built tree, so 'Mosina' is a placeholder default — but the gen-2 sibling roster is the genuine new contribution.

open_questions: Is this tree's 'Moshe' the same as Mojżesz Saul Rapaport of Tarnów? Match on first name and the David/Lota/Shimon downstream → almost certainly yes.
Was Menucha Berisz's mother (in which case the Tarnów cert listing Rywka Schiff is for a second marriage), or is 'Menucha' a Hebrew alias for Rywka Schiff?
Who built this tree? (Likely a family member — Doron / Dana / Daniel / older Rapaport relative). Tool appears Geni or MyHeritage.
Where did the 'Dov' name come from for Berisz? If from family tradition, it confirms Berisz used the Hebrew Dov as a name; if from a guess, it's just a translation.

summary.en: Primary-document confirmation of Rebeka Rapaport née Griffel's parentage and full sibling roster. Names all 10 Griffel siblings (matching Edward Gelles's pedigree) plus 7 Grünfeld co-heirs who were descendants of one of Leizor's daughters who married a Grünfeld (likely Chaya Griffel + Shlomo Gruenfeld per Gelles entry, with Grünfeld = Gruenfeld). Documents the family's pre-war wealth — 127,294 zł property in 1938 is substantial. Property at ul. Śródmieście (central Nadwórna) confirms Leizor's town-center residence.

decoded_fields.company: ZYGMUNT GRIFFEL — Tartaki parowe, Przemysł drzewny, Eksport drzewa (Steam sawmills, timber industry, timber export)
decoded_fields.headquarters: Lwów, ul. Kopernika 5 (additional address ul. Kopernika 55)
decoded_fields.infrastructure: Timber storage facilities at Gdynia and Gdańsk Baltic ports
decoded_fields.1934_35_exports: 60,000 tons to England, worth >5 million złotych
decoded_fields.export_distribution: England 82.8%, Holland 8.3%, Germany 2.9%, Switzerland 2.3%, France 1.9%, other 1.7%

decoded_fields.photo: Headshot of P. Zygm. Griffel; photo of timber being loaded onto ship ('Ładowanie drzewa na statek')

summary.en: Zygmunt Griffel was David Memek's first cousin once removed — son of Dawid Mendel Griffel Sr. (Rebeka's brother, Leizor Griffel's eldest son). This page establishes him as a major Galician-Jewish timber industrialist in 1934-35: 60,000 t/year to England, a five-port operation, with ambitions to brand Polish softwood. The 1938 Muszyna sawmill operating under 'Segal-Żupnik-Wilf' (per Rafał Żebrowski's Holocaust article) is identified in 1938 Przegląd Drzewny as a Zygmunt Griffel front company. Lusia's Muszyna hotel Willa Helin was in Zygmunt's sawmill town — the family-business ecosystem extended from Lwów-Gdynia exports down to Carpathian spa-town milling.

title.en: Zygmunt Griffel — Przegląd Drzewny press feature, 1938 ('Export sets to Palestine')

documents.source_archive: Przegląd Drzewny (Polish Timber Review), 1938 issue. Found and cited by Basia 21 May 2026.

summary.en: Article 'Eksport kompletów do Palestyny' (Export of sets to Palestine) listing Galician-Polish timber firms exporting to the British Mandate. Identifies 'Zygmunt Griffel Lwów (Segal, Żupnik, i Wilf)' — confirming the Muszyna sawmill operating under the holding-name 'Segal-Żupnik-Wilf' (per Żebrowski's Holocaust article) was Zygmunt Griffel's company. This is the key citation that linked Lusia's Muszyna hotel to a Griffel-family timber operation.

title.en: [WITHDRAWN — DIFFERENT FAMILY] Tarnów Yizkor Book — biographies of Ben-Zion Rappaport (pp.296-299)
title.he: ספר טרנוב — ביוגרפיות של בן-ציון רפפורט (עמ' 296-299)
title.pl: Księga Pamięci Tarnowa — biografie Ben-Cyjona Rappaporta (s. 296-299)
title.fr: Livre du Souvenir de Tarnów — biographies de Ben-Zion Rappaport (p. 296-299)

documents.source_archive: JewishGen Yizkor Books — 'Tarnow: The Life and Decline of a Jewish City', Volume I. URL: https://www.jewishgen.org/yizkor/tarnow/tar1_296.html. Two biographical sketches: (1) S.H. Bergman, 'Ben-Zion Rappaport and his book Nature and Spirit' (pp.296-298). (2) Ben-Zion Zangen, 'Ben-Zion Rappaport' (pp.298-299).

summary.en: **PARADIGM-COMPLETING DOCUMENT** — discovered by CC web research 23 May 2026. Establishes that Berisz / Benzion Rapaport (paternal great-grandfather, b. Tarnów 1884) was the published Hebrew philosopher Ben-Zion Rappaport. Documents his entire life trajectory: rabbinic-family origins (both parents from famous rabbinical lines), age-16 arranged marriage to prevent secularization, business failure → private Hebrew teacher in Tarnów → political articles in 'Hamitzpah' about Hasidic figures → forced exile from Tarnów → Kraków poverty → philosophical scholarship (Spinoza, Kant, Ahad Ha'am) → publications in 'Haolam' (Odessa, 1912-14) → three philosophical books (1924, 1936, 1953-posthumous) → Hebrew language professor at Kraków Hebrew Gymnasium → 'death train' deportation from Kraków → murder at Bełżec → manuscript saved by Polish farmer near Bełżec → published 1953 by Mossad Bialik. Names his father as 'R. Moshe Gorlitzer' (= Mojżesz Saul Rapaport per Tarnów birth cert) — 'one of Tarnów's great teachers'.

key_quotes.father: 'R. Moshe Gorlitzer was one of the great teachers in Tarnow, studied in the kloyz [beth-midrash- study-hall] and had an understanding of worldly matters.' (Zangen)
key_quotes.rabbinic_lineage: 'Rappaport was born in Tarnow in 5644 (1884). His parents both descended from famous Rabbis.' (Bergman)
key_quotes.early_marriage: 'After reading Religion and Life by Reuven Asher Broydes at age 18, his father arranged his marriage to prevent him from moving westward.' (Bergman) / 'At age 16, he married.' (Zangen)
key_quotes.career_arc: 'After his wedding he failed as a businessman and turned to teaching... His contributions to this paper made him unpopular with the town leaders and he was forced to leave Tarnow. He settled in Krakow and lived in poverty as a teacher.' (Bergman)
key_quotes.religious_practice: 'Despite being a strict religious Jew, with beard and peiyos, he became a splendid expounder of the world's great philosophers.' (Zangen)
key_quotes.kraków_gymnasium: 'After the Hebrew gymnasium was founded in Krakow, Rappaport worked there as a Hebrew language teacher until World War II's outbreak.' (Zangen)
key_quotes.death: 'The Hitlerite murderers in an evacuation action in Krakow took him in a death train to Belzec where he died a martyr's death.' (Zangen)
key_quotes.manuscript_survival: 'Farmers working railway fields near Belzec discovered his manuscript bundle with a note requesting its delivery to Jerusalem University or to a Jew after the war. A scholar from Jerusalem University later collected it, and the Mossad Bialik published Nature and Spirit in 1953.' (Zangen)

documents.withdrawn_note: 23 May 2026 — WITHDRAWN. CC incorrectly identified the Yizkor philosopher Ben-Zion Rappaport with our Berisz Rapaport. Lusia memoir says Berisz lived in Przemyśl 1939 and was killed there on a railway-bomb accusation, with no philosophy / Sącz / Kraków connection. The Yizkor Ben-Zion was a different individual.

title.en: [WITHDRAWN — DIFFERENT FAMILY] Berisz / Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport — last letter from Nowy Sącz ghetto to his son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport, 25 Sivan / 16 Aug 1942
title.he: בריש / בן-ציון הכהן רפפורט — מכתב אחרון מגטו נוי סונץ לבנו משה הכהן רפפורט, כ"ה סיוון / 16 באוגוסט 1942
title.pl: Berisz / Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport — ostatni list z getta w Nowym Sączu do syna Mosze Hakohena Rappoporta, 25 Siwan / 16 sierpnia 1942
title.fr: Berisz / Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport — dernière lettre du ghetto de Nowy Sącz à son fils Moshe Hacohen Rappoport, 25 Sivan / 16 août 1942

documents.source_archive: Nowy Sącz Yizkor Book pp.601-619 — JewishGen URL https://www.jewishgen.org/Yizkor/Nowy_sacz/now601.html. Letter also bound into the 1953 Mossad Bialik posthumous edition of 'Nature and Spirit' as appendix correspondence. Yad Vashem also holds the letter — https://www.yadvashem.org/he/remembrance/archive/2002/theme/letters/letter10.html

decoded_fields.alternate_date_per_osu: 16 August 1942 (per OSU Hebrew Lexicon — may refer to a different last letter or transmission date)
decoded_fields.recipient: his son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport, in another ghetto
decoded_fields.salutation: To my dear son, his wife and beloved daughters, shalom and great salvation!
decoded_fields.philosophical_content: Discusses overcoming worry and anger; cites Rabbi Yisrael Meir HaCohen (Chofetz Chaim) on guarding the tongue from lies and slander; describes his ongoing manuscript work on theism, body and soul, and the purpose of man. Postscript cites Rabbi Sa'adia Gaon on suffering as rooted in love of life.
decoded_fields.signature: Your father who blesses you with a life of tranquility, rest and everything good. Ben-Zion

summary.en: EXTRAORDINARY DOCUMENT. Berisz's last surviving letter from the Nowy Sącz ghetto, written just weeks before the ghetto's mass deportation to Bełżec (23-28 August 1942). Reveals: (1) Berisz had a son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport (a paternal half-uncle of Dov; new to family knowledge), (2) Moshe Hacohen had a wife + multiple daughters, also perished, (3) Berisz continued writing philosophy even in the ghetto — material from this period found its way into the posthumous Nature and Spirit, (4) Berisz remained a strict religious Jew citing Chofetz Chaim and Sa'adia Gaon, (5) the recipient's marker 'hy"d' (May God avenge his blood) confirms Moshe Hacohen's death.

documents.withdrawn_note: 23 May 2026 — WITHDRAWN. CC incorrectly identified the Yizkor philosopher Ben-Zion Rappaport with our Berisz Rapaport. Lusia memoir says Berisz lived in Przemyśl 1939 and was killed there on a railway-bomb accusation, with no philosophy / Sącz / Kraków connection. The Yizkor Ben-Zion was a different individual.

title.en: [WITHDRAWN — DIFFERENT FAMILY] Nowy Sącz Yizkor Book — chapter on Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport (pp.601-619)
title.he: ספר זכרון נוי-סונץ — פרק על בן-ציון הכהן רפפורט (עמ' 601-619)
title.pl: Księga Pamięci Nowego Sącza — rozdział o Ben-Cyjonie Hakohenie Rappoport (s. 601-619)
title.fr: Livre du Souvenir de Nowy Sącz — chapitre sur Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport (p. 601-619)

documents.source_archive: JewishGen Yizkor Books — Nowy Sącz / Sandz. URL: https://www.jewishgen.org/Yizkor/Nowy_sacz/now601.html. Provides Berisz's biography from the Nowy-Sącz-community perspective, including details about his first marriage and son Moshe Hacohen not present in the Tarnów Yizkor.

summary.en: **FAMILY-DEFINING DOCUMENT**: Provides previously-unknown details about Berisz's first marriage and his son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport. Documents: (1) Berisz lost his mother at age 3 (~1887), (2) at age 13 (~1897) his father R. Moshle Riglitzer moved with him to Nowy Sącz to live with his sister Sarah Mahler, (3) Berisz's first marriage was arranged by his sister Sarah to 'the daughter of a good family from Żmigród' at age 17, (4) his first wife was a 'Woman of Valor' who dealt in fabrics at regional fairs, (5) he had a son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport who married and had daughters, (6) all of them perished in the Holocaust, (7) the last letter from Nowy Sącz ghetto is reproduced in full.

key_quotes.father_riglitzer: His father R' Moshle Riglitzer... was a teacher of boys.
key_quotes.early_orphaning: His mother died when he was three years old.
key_quotes.move_to_sącz: His father moved with him to Sandz (Nowy Sacz), to his only daughter, Sarah, wife of Mordechai Mahler the enlightened maskil, a Zionist and activist.
key_quotes.religious_conflict: He pressured his daughter to remove the 'apikoros' teacher from her home.
key_quotes.first_marriage: She exerted herself and found him a wife, the daughter of a good family from Zmigrod. He was then about seventeen.
key_quotes.first_wife_woman_of_valor: His wife, who was forced to be a Woman of Valor and provide for the family, began to deal in fabrics, and traveled to the fairs in the area.
key_quotes.teaching_zmigrod: Ben-Zion became a teacher in her town of Zemigrod.
key_quotes.move_krakow: He should move to Krakow, a city of scholars and lovers of secular learning.
key_quotes.publications: He began writing philosophical articles for 'HaShiloach', 'Haolam', and 'Hatekufa'.
key_quotes.final_days: In the terrible days he left there for Sandz to join his family... there he died together with all the people.
key_quotes.letter_header: The last letter of the teacher, author, and philosopher, Rabbi Ben-Zion Hacohen Rappoport... to his only son Moshe Hacohen Rappoport, hy"d... Zandzin near Tarnow

title.en: [WITHDRAWN — DIFFERENT FAMILY] Ben-Zion Rappaport — published philosophical works
title.he: בן-ציון רפפורט — ספריו הפילוסופיים
title.pl: Ben-Zion Rappaport — opublikowane dzieła filozoficzne
title.fr: Ben-Zion Rappaport — œuvres philosophiques publiées

works.title: Consciousness and Reality — A Collection of Philosophical Essays
works.note: First book. Manuscript completed earlier — publication delayed 5 years. Possibly Berisz traveled to Vienna in Feb 1924 (per 1924 passport application) for publication-related business.

works.title: Thinkers and Logic
works.note: Second book. Published while teaching at Kraków Hebrew Gymnasium.

works.title: Nature and Spirit: Philosophical Inquiries
works.note: Posthumous — manuscript saved from Bełżec death-train fields by Polish farmers and published 10 years after Berisz's murder. Likely available at: National Library of Israel, Hebrew University Library, Mossad Bialik archives. **High-priority action: Doron should acquire a copy as a family heirloom.**

summary.en: Berisz / Ben-Zion Rappaport's three Hebrew philosophical books — a tangible legacy that survived the Holocaust. He was, per S.H. Bergman's biographical sketch, 'one of those who laid the foundation for philosophical thought in the period of the revival of Hebrew literature' and 'a popularizer of philosophical ideas for Hebrew readers, explaining complex thinkers like Spinoza, Kant, and Ahad Ha'am without condescension.'

summary.en: Describes the Muszyna sawmill (attributed to 'Segal-Żupnik-Wilf' — a holding name for Zygmunt Griffel's Lwów company per 1938 Przegląd Drzewny). The sawmill was converted into a concentration camp during the German occupation. Contains a map of the sawmill (per Basia's chat note 21 May 21:01). Provides timeline of 1939-1940 Jewish exodus from Muszyna.
